goal: Detect any prospect replies to the 112 sent outreach emails using web_fetch of Gmail or SMTP log analysis as CDP-independent fallback.
success_criteria: Definitive yes/no on whether any of 112 sent prospects has replied
If yes: lead_id, excerpt, RED flag. If no: documented that reply window is active with expected timeframe.

slices.task: Detect replies WITHOUT CDP dependency. Step 1: Get latest SMTP send logs for bounce/reply patterns: ls -t /Users/ryleebenson/Desktop/OPENCLAW/workspaces/outreach/artifacts/outreach-drafts/*send* /Users/ryleebenson/Desktop/OPENCLAW/workspaces/outreach/artifacts/*send* 2>/dev/null | head -5 — read the most recent for any reply/bounce notes. Step 2: Check CRM for any stage changes that indicate replies: sqlite3 /Users/ryleebenson/Desktop/OPENCLAW/data/crm.sqlite "SELECT l.id,l.name,l.stage FROM leads l WHERE l.stage IN ('negotiating','won','responded') OR l.notes LIKE '%reply%' OR l.notes LIKE '%responded%'". Step 3: Attempt web_fetch of https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#inbox (may fail due to auth wall — note if so). Step 4: Compile findings into a single yes/no determination. Write to artifacts/missions/reply-check-v2-20260429-1112/bridge-reply.md with: method used, findings, reply status (YES/NO), timeline (first sends ~04:00 UTC, now ~17:00 UTC = 13 hours elapsed, reply window 24-72h still open).

reasoning: Reply detection without CDP — using sqlite3 CRM queries and artifact log analysis. No browser dependency. 112 sends out, 13 hours elapsed since first batch. The reply window is the only variable that changes pipeline state now. Everything else (send more, research, deploy) has been proposed and the cron agents are handling it autonomously.
